Name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Jun 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python versions prior to 3.9.23, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.18, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.13, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.11 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.4.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2024-12718: Allows modifying some le metadata (e.g. last modied) with lter='data' or le permissions (chmod) with lter='tar' of les outside the extraction directory.
- CVE-2025-4138: Allows creating arbitrary symlinks outside the extraction directory during extraction with lter='data'.
- CVE-2025-4330: Allows the extraction lter to be ignored, allowing symlink targets to point outside the destination directory, and the modication of some le metadata.
- CVE-2025-4435: Filtered members not skipped with TarFile.errorlevel = 0
- CVE-2025-4517: Allows arbitrary lesystem writes outside the extraction directory during extraction with lter='data'.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Dec 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.13.11 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1. Note: Fixes have been backported to maintenance branches (3.10, 3.12) but specic patch versions are not yet available.
solution: Solution type: VendorFix Update to version 3.14.1 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.
insight: When reading an HTTP response from a server, if no read amount is specied, the default behavior will be to use Content-Length. This allows a malicious server to cause the client to read large amounts of data into memory, potentially causing out-of-memory (OOM) or other DoS conditions.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Jul 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python versions prior to 3.9.24, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.19, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.14, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.12 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.6.
insight: There is a defect in the CPython 'tarle' module aecting the 'TarFile' extraction and entry enumeration APIs. The tar implementation would process tar archives with negative osets without error, resulting in an innite loop and deadlock during the parsing of maliciously crafted tar archives.

Name: Python Command Injection Vulnerability (Oct 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a command injection vulnerability in the venv module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Command Injection Vulnerability (Oct 2024) - Linux
impact: Python versions prior to 3.9.21, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.16, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.11, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.8 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.1.
insight: A vulnerability has been found in the venv module and CLI where path names provided when creating a virtual environment were not quoted properly, allowing the virtual environment creator to inject commands into virtual environment 'activation' scripts (ie 'source venv/bin/activate'). This means that attacker-controlled virtual environments are able to run commands when the virtual environment is activated. Virtual environments which are not created by an attacker or which aren't activated before being used (ie './venv/bin/python') are not aected.

Name: Python <= 3.12.1 'RecursionError' Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a 'RecursionError' vulnerability.
detection_result: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
NOTE: email.utils.parseaddr is categorized as a Legacy API in the documentation of the Python email package. Applications should instead use the email.parser.BytesParser or email.parser.Parser class.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: The legacy email.utils.parseaddr function allows attackers to trigger 'RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object' via a crafted argument. This argument is plausibly an untrusted value from an application's input data that was supposed to contain a name and an e-mail address.
NOTE: The vendor's perspective is that this is neither a vulnerability nor a bug. The email package is intended to have size limits and to throw an exception when limits are exceeded.

Name: Python <= 2.7.18, 3.x <= 3.12.3 Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a security bypass vulnerability in the e-mail module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python <= 2.7.18, 3.x <= 3.12.3 Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
Note: The vendor mentions, that a patch was included in main branch 3.13. This version is expected to release in October 2024. The patch might be included in the release of version 3.13 or later.
insight: The e-mail module incorrectly parses e-mail addresses which contain a special character. This vulnerability allows attackers to send messages from e-mail addresses that would otherwise be rejected.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Oct 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python versions prior to 3.9.25 and 3.10.x prior to 3.15.0.
Note: Fixes have been backported to maintenance branches (3.10, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14) but specific patch versions are not yet available.
solution: Update to Python 3.9.25, 3.15.0 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x, 3.13.x, 3.14.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.
insight: If the value passed to os.path.expandvars() is user-controlled a performance degradation is possible when expanding environment variables. This vulnerability exhibits quadratic complexity behavior that could be exploited for denial of service attacks.

Name: Python 'ziple' Module Vulnerability (Oct 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a vulnerability in the 'ziple' module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.9.24, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.19, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.14, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.12, 3.13.x prior to 3.13.10 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1.
insight: The 'ziple' module would not check the validity of the ZIP64 End of Central Directory (EOCD) Locator record oset value would not be used to locate the ZIP64 EOCD record, instead the ZIP64 EOCD record would be assumed to be the previous record in the ZIP archive. This could be abused to create ZIP archives that are handled dierently by the 'ziple' module compared to other ZIP implementations. Remediation maintains this behavior, but checks that the oset specied in the ZIP64 EOCD Locator record matches the expected value.

Name: Python Use After Free Vulnerability (May 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an use after free vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Use After Free Vulnerability (May 2025) - Linux
impact: Python prior to version 3.9.23, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.18, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.13, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.11 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.4.
insight: There is an issue in CPython when using bytes.decode(). If you are not using the 'unicode_escape' encoding or an error handler your usage is not aected. To work-around this issue you may stop using the error= handler and instead wrap the bytes.decode() call in a try-except catching the DecodeError.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Jun 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.9.24, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.19, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.14, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.12 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.6.
insight: The html.parser.HTMLParser class had worse-case quadratic complexity when processing certain crafted malformed inputs potentially leading to amplied denial of service.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
Details: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: Python Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (Jan 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an improper input validation vulnerability in the urllib.parse.urlsplit and urlparse standard functions.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (Jan 2025) - Linux
impact: Python versions prior to 3.9.22, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.17, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.12, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.9 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.2.
insight: The Python standard library functions urllib.parse.urlsplit and urlparse accepted domain names that included square brackets which isn't valid according to RFC 3986. Square brackets are only meant to be used as delimiters for specifying IPv6 and IPvFuture hosts in URLs. This could result in differential parsing across the Python URL parser and other specification-compliant URL parsers.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: Allowed HTTP Methods Enumeration
description: Enumerates which HTTP methods are allowed.
detection_result: The following list contains the URLs and corresponding supported HTTP methods.
http://vampi.trabalho_vulnnet:5000/ | HEAD, GET, OPTIONS (obtained via HTTP OPTIONS method)
insight: - Basic HTTP methods: GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, CONNECT, OPTIONS, TRACE

- Extended HTTP methods: ACL, BASELINE-CONTROL, BIND, CHECKIN, CHECK- OUT, COPY, LABEL, LINK, LOCK, MERGE, MKACTIVITY, MKCALENDAR, MKCOL, MKREDIRECTREF, MKWORKSPACE, MOVE, ORDERPATCH, PATCH, PRI, PROPFIND, PROPPATCH, REBIND, REPORT, SEARCH, UNBIND, UNCHECKOUT, UNLINK, UN- LOCK, UPDATE, UPDATEREDIRECTREF, VERSION-CONTROL
log_method: Sends multiple HTTP requests and checks the responses.
- This enumeration script is provided 'as is' (means no support is given) and only providing rudimentary information about the possible enabled methods
- This script doesn't guarantee completeness or full reliability
For more reliable determination of the enabled HTTP methods please inspect the conguration of the web server manually and directly on the target host.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information
log_method: Details: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

Name: Python Detection Consolidation
description: Consolidation of Python detections.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.30.10.2):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 70%
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an an ->swer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.
Details: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

Name: Unknown OS and Service Banner Reporting
description: This VT consolidates and reports the information collected by the following VTs:
- Collect banner of unknown services (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11154)
- Service Detection (unknown) with nmap (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.66286)
- Service Detection (wrapped) with nmap (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.108525)
- OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.105937)
If you know any of the information reported here, please send the full output to the referenced community forum.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Unknown banners have been collected which might help to identify the OS running ->on this host. If these banners containing information about the host OS please -> report the following information to https://forum.greenbone.net/c/vulnerabili ->ty-tests/7:
Identified from: HTTP Server banner on port 5000/tcp
log_method: Details: Unknown OS and Service Banner Reporting

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan. Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE. Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
